title: What are the plug suits made of
display_title: What are the plug suits made of
wikitext:
[]: What are the plug suits made of?             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu May 08, 2014 1:36 pm Short question that I originally posted in the pics thread. I'm thinking that it would be some material akin to Latex, or some type of malleable rubber. It has to be thin, but it's also got to be thick enough to make those sounds that we hear when the pilots tighten their hands. What do you guys think? Note: not a joke thread.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu May 08, 2014 1:38 pm Some kind of advanced material that reacts to an electrical current or the like. It's not any earthly material. Unstable molecules with the properties of memory cloth.

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Thu May 08, 2014 1:59 pm Yeah, something tells me that the guy at NERV who suggested that plugsuits to be worn by teens (and possibly pre-teens, when you consider Asuka and Rei) should be made of latex would probably end up on a sex offenders' register. I think it's some sort of combination of neoprene and kevlar.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu May 08, 2014 2:56 pm Answering to a post from the fanart thread from which the tangent originates : Chuckman wrote: Nothing could be as tight as those suits, yet allow Mari's boobies to bounce and jounce so freely, nor can any such material fail to form cleavage or a single uniboob. Unless it's molded to her exact shape it can't actually be skin-tight and yet allow her chest to move that way.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu May 08, 2014 2:59 pm driftking18594 wrote: You were saying?  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu May 08, 2014 3:16 pm ^ That support the assumption that in-universes, plugsuits are molded exactly to the pilot's body shape. Under that light, I think that the curse of Eva was actually a blessing for the technicians in charge of WILLE's plugsuits : no worries to have that the girls grow up and that the plugsuits have to be replaced, hell even a plugsuit from 14 years ago still fits perfectly!

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu May 08, 2014 3:40 pm How can they be molded to shape if they start out super loose. More importantly, what madman would devise a perfectly fitted bodysuit for a nubile teen with d cups and include no breast support?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu May 08, 2014 3:45 pm Magic and possibly NANOMACHINES. With fictional memory material it would still be a good idea to have an original mold of the host. That's the memory state. The relaxed state would be for putting it on.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu May 08, 2014 3:47 pm Chuckman wrote: How can they be molded to shape if they start out super loose.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu May 08, 2014 3:53 pm I think it’s made out of the same stuff that joins the Eva armor at the joints. Ever noticed that both Eva Units and Eva Pilots make that same rubber bending sound whenever they move around?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu May 08, 2014 4:03 pm That's just Japan's sound editors recycling the same sound effects for almost 50 years now. Watch some mecha or toku/sentai. Doesn't matter if it's 1974, 1994 or 2014, the same sound effect will inevitably crawl up. Anno is also clearly guilty of doing it on purpose. Even the Khara logo sfx is some old ultraman sound iirc. EDIT: Even with magic in-universe stuff it wouldn't make sense from a material properties point of view to use the same stuff for someone 1.5m tall and for someone 80m tall. I would suspect Eva's 2nd skins are their own brand of magic.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu May 08, 2014 4:50 pm Hot glue is only answer.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu May 08, 2014 5:00 pm Xard wrote: Hot glue is only answer.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu May 08, 2014 5:05 pm Xard wrote: Hot glue is only answer.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu May 08, 2014 5:23 pm "Hot glue" should never be said within the context of full body covering, especially when said by someone with the title "Sex God Bastard."

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu May 08, 2014 5:27 pm Evidence on the internet suggests Japan has plenty of experience with covering whole bodies in "hot glue"

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Thu May 08, 2014 5:29 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: I've seen that one before (I've probably seen 99% of all plugsuit cosplay) and I stand by my words. That's not entirely terrible but it's nowhere near good either. The tits and collar area are absolutely atrocious. I've seen the back of it too and the "backpack" is also way off. The cuffs are wrong. The attempt to add bulk where there should be is commendable but in the end a failure. Literally every detail is off in some way. There's plenty of seams where there shouldn't be any. I understand there's practical limits to the material but that's rendered moot when the material chosen was inappropriate in the first place.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu May 08, 2014 5:33 pm Stryker wrote: There is no such material I have ever seen that has all these crinkles that then flatten out when a vacuum is applied.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Thu May 08, 2014 5:34 pm It appears I've made a strawman, then. Is there such a material that shrinks following a pre-determined pattern? If so, can it be used in this manner? I have heard of a concept that is being worked on which is where nanomachines change shape, but these shapes tend to be rigid structures; I do not see them being usable in a case where it has to fit a constantly moving body.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu May 08, 2014 5:40 pm If anybody's seen some of the behind the scenes for Winter Soldier , there were some shots where Bucky's arm was actually a rubber covering. Since we know that the suits (or at least Mari's in NME, and Asuka's in NTE) are in two pieces and clip together before being vacuum-sealed, I stand by the fact that some type of rubber would work. I mean, while they seem like they would be thin, the suits look pretty thick in some shots.
summary: []: What are the plug suits made of?             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu May 08, 2014 1:36 pm Short question that I originally posted in the pics thread. I'm thinking that it would be some material akin to Latex, or some type of malleable rubber. It has to be thin, but it's also got to be thick enough to make those sounds that we hear when the pilots tighten their hands. What do you guys think? Note: not a joke thread.
